page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model and 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates average overall performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The server achieves average ratings in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations, which reached 19.65 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench text processing rated as weak. Large-model LLM inference is also limited, with text generation on a 7B model rated as poor and prompt processing on a 70B model rated as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model offers a high density of memory resources relative to compute power, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core performance. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU model and poor single-core execution speeds, which contrast with the solid, average-tier multi-threaded capabilities and memory bandwidth.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications such as database operations, caching layers, multi-threaded software compilation, and static web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded processing tasks, real-time text processing, or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, including solid multi-threaded decompression and database operations, though single-core performance and text processing are weak. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy tasks, making it suitable for database hosting, caching, and static web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded applications or large language model inference.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. It delivers average multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server with sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of RAM for database workloads.
